meaning: vt.启发,启蒙,教导,授予...知识,开导,<古>照耀
examples: It was an elite that believed its task was to enlighten the multitude.
A few dedicated doctors have fought for years to enlighten the profession.
Instead, she set up Enlighten our Future, a charity that reaches students in the countryside how to write poems.
We hope to enlighten the average fan as to what goes on within these clubs.
Could Jeff Stafeil, CEO of Dura Automotive, a car parts company in Patriarch's portfolio, enlighten me?
